title: How can I create a subitem to all items that are already created?
excerpt:
How can I create a subitem to all items that are already created? 
I mean at once, not manually one by one. 
I already have 685 items…

posts.author: nataliakehat (NataLia)
posts.cookedHtml:
How can I create a subitem to all items that are already created?
I mean at once, not manually one by one.
I already have 685 items…

posts.author: anon29275264 (anon29275264)
posts.cookedHtml:
You could set up a automation recipe to create a subitem when a status changes. Then you can select up to the limit (i think 500?) items and change the status on all of them at once, and it will then create the subitems. You will need to then select the remaining items, but this is pretty trivial compared to one-by-one! This works if your subitems all need the same values.

Now, if each subitem needs different values (from a data source), or values based on the parent, it gets much trickier, and a tool like make.com would be necessary. And the time/ability to create the no-code solution that achieves the desired result. There are also people available for hire to do this for you if you need assistance.

posts.author: nataliakehat (NataLia)
posts.cookedHtml:
Cody, it worked! Thank you so much for the tip!
I needed all the same values, so it was easy with the automation!

posts.author: anon29275264 (anon29275264)
posts.cookedHtml:
Glad it worked!

Don’t forget to remove the automation if you no longer need it - nothing would be worse than someday having 685 more subitems created!
